methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Diode is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Davide Asnaghi and Lenny Khazan that has raised $10M+. The comparable universe spans 9 private companies and 1 public company in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression method, anchored to the company's $10M total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier for progression from Seed to the subsequent stage, yielding an implied valuation of $25M. A peer-median approach could not be executed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable datapoints within the robotics and manufacturing sector; while 11 peers exist in the broader sector, none met the narrow filtering criteria of stage alignment or similar valuation tier required to generate a reliable median.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median estimate of $25M. This range reflects reasonable upside and downside variance around the stage-progression baseline but carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single methodology. The primary limiting factor is the sparse availability of tightly matched comparables at seed stage in this sector. Future revaluation with additional peer data or upon achievement of concrete operational milestones would improve precision.
